platform-labeled.description: Each caption labeled with target platform

input-01.note: Clearly labeled INSTAGRAM and LINKEDIN in bold

input-02.note: Clearly labeled LINKEDIN and TWITTER/X in bold

input-04.note: Clearly labeled INSTAGRAM, LINKEDIN, and TWITTER/X in bold

input-05.note: Vague input; skill correctly declined to produce full output but placeholder caption is labeled Instagram

platform-conventions.description: Platform-specific conventions, not same text reformatted

input-01.note: Instagram uses hook + narrative + link in bio + hashtags; LinkedIn uses professional framing, no hashtags, formal CTA

input-02.note: LinkedIn leads with the technical case study angle; Twitter/X is compressed to key stats and a link

input-03.note: Single platform, appropriately minimal for Instagram sunset content

input-04.note: Instagram uses emotional hook and hashtags; LinkedIn targets policy professionals; Twitter/X is compressed and urgent

input-05.note: Skill declined due to insufficient input; no platform-specific output to evaluate

no-generic-filler.description: No 'check this out', 'so excited to share', etc.

input-05.note: Placeholder deliberately demonstrates the problem of generic output; skill flags it as inadequate

hook-first-line.description: Opening line works as standalone hook

input-01.note: Instagram: 'Delivery riders in three cities are earning below minimum wage.' — clear, specific, stops scrolling. LinkedIn: leads with investigation framing.

input-02.note: LinkedIn: 'A 2,000-year-old ceramic vessel — too fragile to handle, too important to leave undocumented.' — strong contrast hook. Twitter/X: same vessel hook compressed.

input-03.note: 'End of the day. No filter needed.' — minimal but functional for sunset content

input-04.note: Instagram: 'Three families. Two years in hotel rooms. No end date.' — strong rhythmic hook with specifics

input-05.note: Placeholder hook is deliberately generic to illustrate the problem

input-01.note: All 8 hashtags directly related to gig economy, labor rights, investigative journalism — no reach-padding

input-02.note: No hashtags used (LinkedIn and Twitter/X) — appropriate platform convention

input-03.note: 5 hashtags all directly related to sunset/nature photography — minimal and relevant

input-04.note: 9 hashtags covering housing, Dublin, documentary podcast — all topically relevant

input-05.note: No hashtags produced due to insufficient input

summary.notes: Input-05 was deliberately vague/adversarial. The skill correctly identified insufficient input and declined to produce full output, which is the appropriate professional response. All 4 substantive inputs passed all 5 assertions. The skill handles multi-platform, single-platform, minimal-but-valid, and international contexts effectively.
